Service identification.content: Service Dedicated LAN platform Management Unique ID PRD.4.5.003 Service Model Infrastructure management This service is a base service and is not dependant of other layers (and not dependant of other Service Descriptions).

Infrastructure management.content: This Service is the management and administration of a dedicated LAN platform. The Dedicated LAN platform Management is a Cegeka managed service which consist of the monitoring, management, maintenance, supervision, administration and planning of all functions relating to infrastructure and components of local LAN network. The Managed LAN service unburdens the Customer from the day-to-day management of the LAN infrastructure. Cegeka manages the LAN network on a remote (i.e. off-site) basis from our operation center, proactively fixing faults and keeping the service running. Cegeka provides reports on the status of the managed  network and informs the Customer about incidents that occur.

Standard Service features.content: The following standard features are included in this Service: The scope of the Managed LAN service covers the management of all active and manageable physical LAN components which are subject to this service. The demarcation line is the starting point of the WAN infrastructure of the WAN service provider. The WAN connectivity (incl. routers) and management of the servers and desktops is not part of this service. These functions can be delivered via other Cegeka services. The managed LAN equipment is owned by the Customer. Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the infrastructure in or der to perform the Service. Cegeka advises in the setup of the Infrastructure in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Infrastructure and in line with the best practices of the vendor. This may be different for each Customer environment (DTAP - Development, Test, Acceptance, Production and it’s variants). Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer. Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Infrastructure and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows.  Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Infrastructure. Automation is applied where possible. Proactive management Housekeeping includes following regular checks: Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA. Patching The service includes a maximum of 2 patch cycles (per environment) per year for the managed Infrastructure. Patching is done in accordance to the operational Patch Plan. Life Cycle Management Cegeka monitors the Infrastructure and notifies the Customer timely when they are subject to end-of-life. This includes, if applicable, the extension or replacement of the Infrastructure. Cegeka also notifies the Customer upon newer versions of the Infrastructure in order to plan the update of the platform in time within the followed (relative) version (for example N-1). Life Cycle Updates will be planned in accordance with the agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis. The customer will be informed at least 6 months in advance to properly plan the upgrade, as significant customer effort is required in application testing. Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Infrastructure. Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service. Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management.  Management of technical users The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies. Detection of security incidents The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. SIEM Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request. Underlaying Platform hardening Not applicable for this Service. Backup and restore of the network component configurations is part of the Cegeka management. Configuration backup are always taken upon change of the configuration. Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals). Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals). Acquisition of the LAN devices. Please note that Cegeka needs to approve the manageability of the LAN devices prior to being able to manage them to ensure Cegeka knowledge of these devices. Removal and destruction of the devices at end-of-use.

Optional Service features.content: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. The following optional features are possible with the Service:

Requests.content: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). request type Description Lead time Service Requests (no Service Requests for this Service) Normal Changes Any other request. Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.

Out of scope.content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service. Certain LAN Switches have network modules or network management functionality (e.g. load balancing, firewall, blade server, virtual chassis, etc.). The management of such functionality is not included in the LAN Service.

Obligatory.content: The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:

Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptio.content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Cross-Functional Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License included in service applies to (none specific)

Customer owned Licenses and/or subscript.content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are not included in this Service. It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License description/remarks Platform licenses When applicable, the Customer is responsible for the platform licenses for the used functionality. License compliancy Customer has to share the available licenses with Cegeka. Cegeka will assist in customer license compliance by providing knowledge and reporting on use of licenses and license requirements. Cegeka ensures to implement or activate features that are only in-line with the licenses owned by the customer.

Conditions.content: This service supports the following products:

Service delivery location.content: This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.

Service Reporting.content: Monthly reporting will be provided to inform on the availability and meeting the SLA’s and to provide insights on the capacity and expected extensions required. Specific reports are provided:

Data classification.content: Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service. Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service.

Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer.content: This service is delivered with: Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues): (none) Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party): (none)

Adherence to Cross-Functional Services.content: The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services n/a The Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Service Window.content: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Service Level.content: Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. The service is handled according to the cross-functional services. The availability of the service is guaranteed by the incident handling SLA’s (resolve times).

Standard Service Billing.content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. ID Component Billing element BILLING UNIT SKU.4.5.003. L01 LAN management Dedicated LAN management Amount/month (Amount is subject to changes in the total managed equipment) Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a monthly basis: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Change Request Billing.content: Changes are billed on a monthly basis. Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact. Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.
